questions.question: How do you render a list of items in React?
What is the Virtual DOM?
options: A direct copy of the browser's DOM
A lightweight, in-memory representation of the real DOM
questions.answer: A lightweight, in-memory representation of the real DOM

questions.question: Why is the 'key' prop important when rendering lists in React?
options: It helps React identify which items have changed, been added, or removed
It acts as a unique ID for the database
questions.answer: It helps React identify which items have changed, been added, or removed

questions.question: How do you conditionally render a component in React?
options: Using if/else statements or the ternary operator
questions.answer: Using if/else statements or the ternary operator

questions.question: What is the 'children' prop in React?
options: A prop that passes data to child components
A special prop used to pass elements directly into a component's output
An array of all child components rendered in the DOM
A method to count the number of child components
questions.answer: A special prop used to pass elements directly into a component's output

questions.question: How can you prevent default behavior in a React form submission?
Which of the following is true about React state?
options: State updates should be done using the state updater function
State is accessible globally by default
questions.answer: State updates should be done using the state updater function

questions.question: What does ReactDOM.createRoot do in React 18?
options: Creates a root to display React components inside a browser DOM node
Sets up the global state container
questions.answer: Creates a root to display React components inside a browser DOM node
